core_mechanism: A diffusion-driven (Turing-type) finite-wavelength instability in coupled reactive fields that is sustained by intrinsic stochastic fluctuations: noise is amplified into a patterned state at a selected nonzero wavenumber even when the deterministic dynamics are stable, and the resulting long-wavelength density fluctuations are anomalously suppressed (window number variance decaying as 1/R rather than as the window volume).
brief_reason: The abstract reports that demographic noise generates stochastic Turing patterns in a deterministically stable reaction-diffusion system and that temporal integration yields an intensive number variance approaching a finite floor as 1/R near the instability, i.e. intrinsic fluctuations drive a diffusion-induced finite-scale instability whose fluctuation spectrum is suppressed at large scales (hyperuniformity).
